observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:
(i) submit BRS on a monthly basis per bank account in accordance with Section 3.2
of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996; (ii) implement stricter validation
procedures, conduct thorough reconciliations before finalizing reports; (iii) enhance
oversight mechanisms to ensure the accuracy and reliability of financial records; and
(iv) prepare an adjusting entry for the Cash-in-Bank account amounting to
₱5,000.00, facilitate the closure of the PNB account, and ensure proper
documentation of the account’s closure.

We recommended and Management agreed that the (a) Municipal Accountant
(i) issue demand letters to all concerned AOs for them to liquidate their cash advances
by submitting complete and valid settlement documents or refunding applicable
portion of the cash advances, as necessary (ii) monitor the schedule of cash advances
granted to different Accountable Officers to know when these cash advances are due
for liquidation and cause the withholding of salary, if necessary (iii) post timely in the
GL the liquidations from Advances to Officers and Employees and Advances to SDO
Accounts; and (iv) reconcile monthly the Report of Cash Advances and the GL. We
also recommend that Management (b) (i) stop the practice of granting cash advances
to employees and officials with outstanding/unliquidated cash advances (ii) require a
certification from the Municipal Accountant before granting new cash advance and
(iii) designate other AOs and separately manage cash advances according to its
purpose; (c) AOs (i) liquidate their cash advances as soon as the purpose for which it
was given has been served, specially the unliquidated cash advances which are
outstanding for many years.

3.3 We recommended and Management agreed that the: (a) Municipal Treasurer
(i) furnish the updated RPTAR/ Taxpayer’s index card to Municipal Accountant at
every beginning of the year for recording and adjustment purposes; and (ii) intensify
and strategize tax collection efforts from taxpayers such as deployment of demand
letters, posting of notice of delinquency in conspicuous places and tapping the
assistance of the barangay officials for tax campaign to reach out to a larger area; and
(b) Municipal Accountant (i) establish RPT and SET receivables sourced from
the list duly certified by the Treasury Office at the beginning of the year; (ii) exclude
those who made advance payments during the previous year in the total amount to
be established as RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year; (iii) monitor the
unearned revenue for collections from advance payments in the previous year and
make necessary adjustments to recognize the realized/earned revenue at the
beginning of the subsequent year; and (iv) promptly make the necessary adjustments
as soon as possible whenever a misstatement is discovered.

4.6 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant (i)
establish a periodic review and validation of receivable accounts to promptly identify
discrepancies or unsupported balances; (ii) henceforth, consider the write-off of
dormant receivables for more than 10 years in the books of accounts, in accordance
with the procedures set forth by COA Circular No. 2016- 005; (iii) recognize only the
final and executory audit disallowances and charges in the account, Receivables –
Disallowances/Charges; (iv) update the records on the account Receivables –
Disallowances/Charges and reconcile with the Audit Team’s records in order to make the
necessary adjustment in the Agency’s books; (v) continue identifying the reconciling items
between the reciprocal accounts Due from/to Other Funds and effect the adjustments in the
accounting records, as necessary; and (vi) institute a thorough monitoring of these
reciprocal accounts by ensuring that entries made/posted from one account has a parallel
recognition/posting to the other.

5.6 We recommended and Management agreed that the Accounting Section (i)
ensure that all material and relevant information pertaining to the Receivables
account is clearly and adequately disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statement,
in compliance with the requirements of IPSAS 1; (ii) provide details on the efforts made
to reconcile these accounts, including any limitations or constraints faced, and (iii)
explain the potential financial impact or risks associated with these balances, including
any provisions or allowances made, if any.

6.24 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to require the
Accounting Office and the Supply Officer to: (i) immediately maintain the Stock
Cards (SC) and Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC) for all LGU inventories; (ii) conduct a
periodic reconciliation of the two records to monitor agency assets properly; (iii)
conduct a complete semi-annual physical count of inventories and submit the
corresponding RPCI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year in
accordance with MNGAs for LGUs; (iv) maintain a Summary of Supplies and
Materials Issued (SSMI) to serve as a basis for preparing Journal Entries to record
the supplies and materials issued; (v) maintain a Waste Material Report to ensure
transparency and accountability in the replacements and disposal of waste materials;
and (vi) conform with the implementing guidelines of COA Circular 2022-004 by
properly accomplishing and maintaining all required forms related to semi-
expendable properties. In addition, (vii) assign the responsibility for generating and
safekeeping of ICS to the Supply/Property Section; and (viii) establish and maintain
an organized filing system to facilitate efficient tracking, retrieval, and verification of
custodianship.

7.28 We recommended and Management agreed that the Inventory Committee,
Accounting Office, and the Property Officer, as the case may be, : (i) conduct a
complete annual physical count of PPEs and submit the corresponding RPCPPE in
accordance with Section 124 of MNGAS for LGUs; (ii) maintain the Property Cards
and PPE Ledger Cards for all LGUs properties; (iii) observe the periodic
reconciliation of the two records to monitor agency’s assets properly; (iv) maintain
an Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property to properly document
and track the unserviceable assets of the Municipality; (v) conform with the
implementing guidelines of Section 259 of GAM Vol. 1 for LGUs by assigning the
responsibility for generating and safekeeping PAR to the Property Section; (vi) and
establish and maintain an organized filing system to facilitate efficient tracking,
retrieval, and verification of custodianship; and (vii) implement the One-Time
Cleansing of the PPE account balances in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-
006 to address discrepancies between recorded balances and actual physical assets.

8.8 We recommended and Management agreed that the Accounting Office (i)
make the necessary adjustments pertaining to the omitted depreciation for the Water
Supply Systems, Power Supply Systems, Books, Work/Zoo Animals, and Other PPE;
(ii) consistently provide yearly depreciation of PPEs applying the accounting
estimates adopted by the LGU and relative issuances by the Commission on Audit, as
necessary; and (iii) establish internal controls to ensure depreciation is consistently
and correctly calculated and recorded in future periods.

9.8 We recommended and Management agreed that the Accounting Office : (i) re-
evaluate the accounting treatment applied to the Public Market in accordance with
IPSAS 17 and related section 263 of the GAM Vol.1 for LGUs, by determining the
recoverable service amount of the entire Public market to determine the appropriate
impairment loss; and (ii) ensure that all significant events affecting asset valuation
are adequately disclosed in the Notes to the FS to enhance the accuracy, transparency
and reliability of financial reports.

10.7 We recommended and Management agreed that the Accounting Section
maintain and regularly update the CIPLC for all ongoing construction projects of the
Municipality, using the format prescribed in the GAM for LGUs to ensure efficient
asset management and enhance financial reporting accuracy.

11.15 We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant (i)
prepare and maintain SLs per creditor as necessary to support the balances
presented in the GL and financial statements; and (ii) perform a thorough
reconciliation of the payable accounts totaling ₱64,712,061.55 as of December 31,
2024; (iii) exhaust all remedies to gather the details and supporting documents needed
for the identification of the amounts of dormant and long-outstanding payables to
facilitate the reversion and/or write-off of such in accordance with the provisions of
COA-DBM JC No. 1, s. 2021 and COA Circular No. 2016-005; (iv) assign a qualified
personnel to update and monitor the SLs regularly, ensuring all transactions are
recorded timely and accurately; (v) revisit and ensure the submission of complete
supporting documents to substantiate the derecognition of the ₱7,015.65 under the
Due to GOCCs account in compliance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 to validate
that the liability qualifies for write-off; and (vi) ensure that all material and relevant
information pertaining to the Payables account is clearly and adequately disclosed in
the Notes to the Financial Statement, in compliance with the requirements of IPSAS
1.

12.16 We recommended and Management agreed to: (i) immediately review and
align its accounting procedures with the provisions of COA Circular 2024-009 to
ensure the proper recognition, measurement, and reporting of employee benefits in
accordance with IPSAS 39-Employee Benefits; (ii) adopt the prescribed accounting
entries for the initial set-up, accrual, and settlement of Terminal Leave Benefits, as
illustrated in the Circular; (iii) ensure that all transactions are supported by
appropriate supporting documents, thereby discontinuing the practice of directly
posting entries to the GL without sufficient audit trail; (iv) conduct a thorough
reassessment and recalculation of the terminal leave balances using the proper
methodology outlined in the circular to correct any discrepancies; (v) conduct regular
reconciliation between the HRO’s leave records and the balances recorded in
accounting books to validate the completeness and accuracy of the reported Terminal
Leave Payable and Benefits; and (vi) establish and implement a systematic process to
ensure that employee leave records are up-to-date, regularly monitored, and properly
reconciled to ensure that leave balances are accurate, complete, and reliable.

13.8 We recommended and Management agreed that the (i) process owners of the
Local Roads Asset Management System based on COA Circular 2015-008 to be
mindful of their duties laid down in the said Circular; (ii) Supply/Property Officer
come up with the Local Road Inventory and Road Map and Local Road Network
Property Card for all roads under the Agency’s jurisdiction; and (iii) Accounting
Office disclose information on Road Networks in the Notes to the FS in accordance
with COA Circular 2015-008.

14.6 We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Development
Council (LDC) continue to (i) prioritize projects that promote inclusive growth and
sustainable development within barangay communities on an ongoing basis and (ii)
ensure the active and continuous engagement of barangay stakeholders in identifying
and selecting socio-economic projects to be funded and implemented through the
Development Fund (DF).

15.12 We recommended and Management agreed that the (i) Local Chief Executive
continue upholding its mandate in promoting local development by appropriating
20% of its annual IRA pursuant to the provisions of the LGC and DILG-DBM JMC
No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017; (ii) Engineering Office and the Municipal
Planning and Development Office (MPDO) ensure the timely implementation and
delivery of all upcoming projects for Calendar Year 2025 and subsequent budget
years, while strictly adhering to the legal requirements and guidelines governing each
project; (iii) Planning and Engineering Office establish a consistent on-site
supervision by conducting frequent and unannounced visits to ensure ongoing
adherence to project requirements; (iv) Engineering Office assign a dedicated LGU
vehicle specifically prioritized for project monitoring purposes; (v) Engineering
Office issue formal notices to the contractors to promptly correct all deviations from
the approved plans and specifications; and (vi) Engineering Office to ensure that
projects are inspected first before accepting/turn-over of projects from contractors.

16.4 We recommended and Management agreed that the (i) BAC together with the
TWG carefully review the documents submitted by the bidders and ensure that they
comply as prescribed by R.A. 9184 and its 2016 revised IRR; (ii) Municipal Engineer
ensure that project contractors observe the proper size and details to be provided on
project signboards including the update thereof to reflect the actual project status in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 and; (iii)
Accounting Office check and ensure the completeness of documents before effecting
payment.

17.22 We recommended and Management agreed to: (i) enforce the submission of
Liquidation Reports (LRs) for all procurement transactions made through cash
advances and submit them to the Audit Team to ensure transparency and
accountability in fund utilization, as mandated under RA 10121 and related COA
guidelines; (ii) ensure that all expenses charged to the LDRRMF are directly related
to disaster risk reduction and management activities as outlined in the Local DRRM
Plan. Any expense not fully aligned with these objectives should be avoided, justified,
or corrected to prevent misuse of funds; (iii) submit immediately the Disbursement
Vouchers (DVs) with their supporting documents pertaining to the noted transaction;
and (iv) require the submission of all disbursement vouchers supporting DRRM
expenditures within the reglementary period as prescribed by COA Circular 2009-
006 to provide proper audit trails and facilitate monitoring.

18.5 We recommended and Management agreed that the (a) MSWMB (i) prioritize
the review/update of the current SWMP or to expedite the formulation of a new
SWMP to ensure its sustainability, viability and effectiveness; (ii) prepare Long-term
Action Plan and Annual Work Plan and Budget showing the SWM activities to be
implemented and the financial counterpart for each SWM activity; and (iii) continue
to improve the work of the monitoring and evaluation team and require them to
prepare Progress Reports in line with the Annual Work Plan and the SWM Plan; (b)
Management continue to (i) provide the necessary support to the MENRO especially
to enhance the existing operation and to implement additional methods of processing
waste materials in the MRF to further reduce residual wastes; and (ii) initiate
discussions with TARELCO to assess the power capacity and explore options for
upgrading the power supply infrastructure to support the large-capacity composter’s
demand.

19.13 We recommended and Management agreed that the (a) LCE continuously and
constantly monitor, intensify and strategize collection efforts from taxpayers such as
conducting Tax Campaign and sending payment advisory or notice to taxpayers
before the payment period or deadlines and the like; (b) MTO (i) request from the
Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) the list of all BIR registered business
establishments in Camiling, Tarlac with their declared gross sales; and (ii) conduct
the examination of books of accounts and pertinent records in accordance with
Section 171 of the LGC, especially businesses/taxpayers who failed to submit their
ITR; (c) BPLO remind the businesses/taxpayers on the submission of certified
photocopies of their ITR; and (d) MAO continue (i) record each collections to the
proper accounts; and (ii) reconcile with the MTO whenever a discrepancy or
inconsistency is discovered in the recording of collections.

20.10 We recommended and Management agreed to (a) continue to capacitate the
GFPS and concerned officials with the mechanisms and processes in mainstreaming
GAD perspective in the LGU by being well-informed and updated of GAD-related
issuances and/or attending training, conferences, symposia administered by the PCW,
DILG, or other NGAs directly involved in GAD concerns; (b) GFPS (i) continuously
update/improve its GAD database and try to integrate other key information such as,
but not limited to, date of birth or age of resident; (ii) administer the use of GeRL
assessment tool in order to measure the LGU’s level of gender-responsiveness as an
organization and in the delivery of its basic services; and (iii) utilize the HGDG tool
and PIMME, as necessary, whenever the LGU plans to attribute a portion or the
whole of the program’s budget to the GAD budget supporting gender-responsive
PPAs.

21.17 We recommended and the Management concurred that the LSB chaired by
the Local Chief Executive (LCE) (i) improve collaboration and abide with the budget
formulation process outlined by DepED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017
with complete documentation as to actual/verified collections as a basis to estimate
annual SEF Budget and comprehensive list of requests from each school sourced
from SIPs; (ii) replace the old forms and present the SEF Budget by program, activity
and project (P/A/P), by allotment class and per school using SEF Budget Preparation
Form No. 1 (Annex A) of the Joint Circular; (iii) disseminate the guidelines, train
staff and improve the process in order to provide the details of the utilization report
as prescribed by Accountability Forms outlined in Joint Circular No. 1; and iv)
ensure that SEF utilization information is updated and displayed in three
conspicuous public places as mandated by the Joint Circular which could include
locations such as community centers, libraries, public market and schools, in addition
to the Municipal Hall's Disclosure Board.

22.7 We recommended and the Management concurred that the LSB chaired by
the LCE (i) consolidate the quarterly and annual reports into a single comprehensive
utilization report that will integrate a clear and holistic view of the utilization
performance of the LSB and LGU on SEF; and (ii) utilize the records coming from
the Local Treasury and Budget Office to have a well-defined and documented basis
for the annual SEF Budget allocation, ensuring that the amount is based on realistic
revenue projections.

23.10 We recommended and Management agreed that the (a) Municipal Accountant
(i) prepare the corresponding adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the
affected accounts; (ii) record transactions based on its nature/substance and
according to the definition of accounts under the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local
Government Units (RCALGU); (iii) secure from the DepEd accountant a certification
confirming that all expenses chargeable against the SEF are net of the budgetary
provision for the same or related item(s) in the DepEd budget; and (iv) brief the LSB
on documentary requirements for government transactions and ensure that all
disbursements are supported with complete documentation and that necessary
data/information are provided before payment.

24.11 We recommended and the Management agreed to (i) submit immediately the
DVs with their supporting documents pertaining to the noted transactions; and (ii)
implement a strict policy requiring all transactions to be supported by complete
documentation, as mandated by Section 43(4) of Presidential Decree No. 1445; (iii)
provide training to staff involved in records management to ensure they understand
the importance of complete documentation and compliance with regulation; and (iv)
implement clear consequences for non-compliance with documentation requirements
and timely submission to encourage adherence to policies.

26.6 We recommended and the Management agreed to (i) further enhance fiscal
responsibility and transparency; (ii) always reconcile and provide details on the
difference between taxes withheld and remitted; (iii) strengthen internal controls and
staff training on tax procedures, and adopt systems for accurate tax computation and
timely remittance.

27.6 We recommended, and the LCE has agreed to implement the following
measures to ensure proper financial management and compliance with regulatory
requirements to: (i) continue the prompt and complete remittance of loan
amortizations as well as mandatory contributions to government agencies such as the
Government Service Insurance System (GSIS), Pag-IBIG Fund, and PhilHealth.
These payments should be made within the prescribed deadlines to avoid penalties or
lapses in coverage, thereby safeguarding the benefits and entitlements of all eligible
employees; (ii) exert additional and diligent effort in the collection and review of all
pertinent documents related to the subject accounts. This includes conducting a
thorough analysis and reconciliation of the inter-agency payables to establish and
verify the accuracy and legitimacy of the recorded balances; and (iii) ensure that all
due premiums are remitted immediately upon confirmation and when sufficient
evidence supports the obligation.

28.4 We recommended and the LCE concurred that the Municipal Accountant and
other concerned personnel enforce the immediate settlement of disallowances in
accordance with Section 9.4 of the 2009 RRSA.

We recommended and the Local Chief
Executive (LCE) agreed for the Municipal
Accountant: i) submit BRS on a monthly
basis per bank account in accordance with
Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011
dated October 2, 1996; ii) prepare an
adjusting entry for the Cash-in-Bank and
recognize an Accounts Receivable account
in the amount of ₱66,000.00; iii) reconcile
the discrepancy between the amounts in the
Current and Savings Accounts as presented
in the FS with those presented in the BRS,
a thorough review of both sets of records is
essential; iv) effectively utilize the funds
totaling ₱62,358,533.19 from the high-yield
savings account of the Municipality towards
programs/projects that would redound to the
benefit of its constituents; and v) prepare an
adjusting entry for the Cash-in-Bank account
amounting to ₱5,000.00, facilitate the
closure of the PNB accounts, and ensure
proper documentation of the account’s
closure.

require the Accounting Office and the
Supply Officer to: (i) maintain the Stock
Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards for all

and observe the periodic reconciliation of the
two records to monitor agency’s assets

Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property

We recommended and the LCE agreed to
instruct the Accounting Office to (i) make
the necessary adjustments pertaining to the

Work/Zoo Animals, and Other PPE; and (ii)

PPEs applying the accounting estimates
adopted by the LGU and relative issuances
by the Commission on Audit, as necessary.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to
instruct the Accounting Office to assess and
recognize an impairment loss and make the
necessary adjustments to uphold
transparency and accuracy in the
Municipality’s financial statements.

We recommended and the LCE agreed that
Accounting Section maintain and regularly
update the CIPLC for all ongoing
construction projects of the Municipality to
ensure efficient asset management and
enhance financial reporting accuracy.

We recommended and the LCE agreed for
the Municipal Accountant to (i) exhaust all
remedies to gather the supporting documents
needed for the receivables noted; (ii)
henceforth, consider the write-off of dormant
receivables for more than 10 years in the
books of accounts, in accordance with the
procedures set forth by COA Circular No.
2016- 005; (iii) facilitate the liquidation of
Advances for Payroll account and continue
to trace the erroneous posting in the account
and effect any adjustments, as necessary; (iv)
recognize only the final and executory audit
disallowances and charges in the account,
Receivables – Disallowances/Charges; (v)
update the records on the account
Receivables – Disallowances/Charges and
reconcile with the Audit Team’s records in
order to make the necessary adjustment in the
Agency’s books; (vi) continue identifying the
reconciling items between the reciprocal
accounts Due from/to Other Funds and
effect the adjustments in the accounting
records, as necessary; and (vii) institute a
thorough monitoring of these reciprocal
accounts by ensuring that entries
made/posted from one account has a parallel
recognition/posting to the other.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to
enjoin the Municipal Accountant to (i)
prepare and maintain SLs per creditor as
necessary to support the balances presented
in the GL and financial statements; and (ii)
Fully exhaust all remedies to gather the
details and supporting documents needed
for the identification of the amounts of
dormant and long-outstanding payables to
facilitate the reversion and/or write-off of
such in accordance with the provisions of
COA-DBM JC No. 1, s. 2021 and COA Circular
No. 2016-005, and (iii) ensure that all
financial transactions are accurately
recorded and reviewed before posting to the
General Ledger.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to
instruct the a) Municipal Accountant to i)
always secure a copy of MOA before
recognizing equipment and/or facilities
funded by inter-agency transfers; ii) make
the necessary adjusting entry on the
improper recognition of the two vehicles
procured under KADIWA Financial grant
Assistance Program; b) Municipal Treasurer
to exert remedies as countermeasure to
determine the source of fund deposited in the
LGUs accounts in the absence of NADAI but
not limited to making confirmations from
Landbank; c) Officers and employees in-
charge of the implementation of projects
funded by other agencies to continue the
timely implementation of projects based on
the terms and conditions of the SAs to ensure
the efficient delivery of public service to the
intended beneficiaries; and d) Municipal
Accountant and Budget Officer to (i)
maintain the good practice of prompt
liquidation of fund transfers including the
immediate refund of unutilized balance of
the said funds; and (ii) update the records of
all fund transfers from different agencies for
proper monitoring and to establish the
reliability of the year-end balance of the
accounts.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to
require the (a) BAC, together with the
Technical Working Group (TWG), i) to take
note/submit the lacking documents such as
the BAC resolution declaring SCRB instead
of LCRB, post-qualification evaluation
reports, posting of Warranty Security, all
under the provisions of R.A.9184 and its IRR
; ii) be apprised of the various intricacies of
bidding and apply them accordingly to
enhance the credibility of bidding process;
and (iii) ensure that project contractors
observe the proper size and details to be
provided on project signboards including the
update thereof to reflect the actual project
status in accordance with COA Circular No.
2013-004 dated January 30, 2013; and (b)
Accounting Office to check and ensure the
completeness of documents before effecting
payment.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to
instruct the a) Municipal Accountant and
Budget Officer to (i) maintain/update the
records of all fund transfers from different
agencies for proper monitoring and to
establish the reliability of the year-end
balance of the accounts; and (ii) exhaust all
remedies to gather the details and supporting
documents needed for the identification of
the amounts of dormant long-outstanding
liabilities to facilitate the reversion and/or
write -off.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (i)
instruct the process owners of the Local Roads
Asset Management System based on COA
Circular 2015-008 to be mindful of their
duties laid down in the said Circular; (ii)
require the Supply/Property Officer to come up
with the Local Road Inventory and Road Map
and Local Road Network Property Card for all
roads under the Agency’s jurisdiction; and
(iii) require the Accounting Office to disclose
information on Road Networks in the Notes to
the FS in accordance with COA Circular 2015-
008.

We recommended and the LCE agreed for
the Municipal Accountant a) maintain the
punctual submission of documents relating
to CF; b) provide and clearly state in the DV
the duration of implementation of the projects
so that if the implementation of the project will
extend to more than three months, additional
amount may be granted only after liquidation
of the previous cash advance; and c) employ a
more efficient and timely release of cash
advance for CF so as not to derail the prompt
accomplishment and implementation of
projects under the said fund.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (i)
submit immediately the DVs with their
supporting documents pertaining to the
noted transactions; and (ii) strictly comply
with the submission of DVs and supporting
documents within the reglementary period as
prescribed by COA Circular 2009-006.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a)
continue upholding its mandate in promoting
local development by appropriating 20% of
its annual NTA pursuant to the provisions of
the LGC and DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1
dated February 22, 2017 and JMC No. 01
dated November 04, 2020; and (b) Engineering
Office and MPDO ensure timely delivery of
the projects for CY 2023 and for every budget
year, taking into account the legal rudiments
of every project; (c) the Local Development
Council (LDC), (i) continue to give precedence
to projects focusing on inclusive growth and
sustainable development of communities at
the barangay level; and (ii) maintain the
proactive involvement of the barangays thru
the LDC in identifying socio-economic
projects to be implemented out of the DF.

We recommended and the LCE agreed (a)
require the BAC, together with the TWG, to
prepare the BAC resolution declaring Single
Calculated and Responsive Bidder (SCRB)
if there is only one responsive bidder as
provided by Section 36 of IRR of R.A. 9184;
(b) ensure that all required postings in
PhilGEPS as mentioned in the Revised IRR
are complied within the period prescribed.
Moreover, attach a printed copy of all
postings to the related disbursement
vouchers; (c) demand from the
representative of the contractors a notarized
formal document to establish that they are
really an agent or an authorized
representative of the winning bidder; (d)
Fully conduct the post-qualification exercise
through significant verification based on the
documents under examination so as to
establish the integrity of documents and to
detect misrepresentation; (c) require the
Engineering Office to ensure that project
contractors observe the proper size and
details to be provided on project signboards
including the update thereof to reflect the
actual project status in accordance with COA
Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30,
2013; and (d) instruct the Accounting Office
to check and ensure the completeness of
documents and exert the proper diligence to
discern that the documents submitted by the
contractors are legitimate before effecting
payment.

We reiterated our previous year’s
recommendation and the LCE agreed to
instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) issue
demand letters to all concerned AOs for
them to liquidate their cash advances by
submitting complete and valid settlement
documents or refunding applicable portion
of the cash advances, as necessary; and (ii)
establish a thorough monitoring of cash
advances granted to different Accountable
Officers by making a schedule when these
cash advances are due for liquidation. We
also recommended the Accounting Office to
reconcile regularly the GL and PRSCA and
settle the discrepancy amounting to
₱892,217.68.

We recommended and the LCE agreed that
the Management (a) direct the MSWMB to

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed for the Municipal Accountant: i) submit BRS on a monthly basis per bank account in accordance with Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996; ii) prepare an adjusting entry for the Cash-in-Bank Account and recognize an Accounts Receivable account in the amount of ₱66,000.00;  iii) reconcile the discrepancy between the amounts in the Current and Savings Accounts as presented in the FS with those presented in the BRS, a thorough review of both sets of records is essential; iv) effectively utilize the funds totaling ₱62,358,533.19 from the high-yield savings account of the Municipality towards programs/projects that would redound to the benefit of its constituents; and v) prepare an adjusting entry for the Cash-in-Bank account amounting to ₱5,000.00, facilitate the closure of the PNB accounts, and ensure proper documentation of the account’s closure.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Accounting Office and the Supply Officer to: (i) maintain the Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards for all LGU inventories and observe the periodic reconciliation of the two records to monitor agency assets properly; (ii) conduct a complete semi-annual physical count of inventories and submit the corresponding RPCI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year in accordance with Section 124 of the MNGAs for LGUs; (iii) maintain a Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to serve as a basis for preparing Journal Entries to record the supplies and materials issued; and (iv) maintain a Waste Material Report to establish transparency regarding the legitimacy of replacements and disposal of waste materials.

We recommended and the LCE agreed that the Inventory Committee, Accounting Office, and the Property Officer, as the case may be, to: (i) conduct a complete annual physical count of PPEs and come up with the RPCPPE in accordance with Section 124 of MNGAS for LGUs; (ii) conduct an investigation if there are missing PPEs and if warranted establish who are the accountable officers for their loss by collating all the Property Acknowledgment Receipt (PAR) on file; (iii) maintain the Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards for all LGUs properties and observe the periodic reconciliation of the two records to monitor agency’s assets properly; and (iv) maintain an Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property to properly document and track the unserviceable assets of the Municipality.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Accounting Office to (i) make the necessary adjustments pertaining to the omitted depreciation for the Water Supply Systems, Power Supply Systems, Books, Work/Zoo Animals, and Other PPE; and (ii) consistently provide yearly depreciation of PPEs applying the accounting estimates adopted by the LGU and relative issuances by the Commission on Audit, as necessary.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Accounting Office to assess and recognize an impairment loss and make the necessary adjustments to uphold transparency and accuracy in the Municipality’s financial statements.

We recommended and the LCE agreed that Accounting Section maintain and regularly update the CIPLC for all ongoing construction projects of the Municipality to ensure efficient asset management and enhance financial reporting accuracy.

We recommended and the LCE agreed for the Municipal Accountant to (i) exhaust all remedies to gather the supporting documents needed for the receivables noted; (ii) henceforth, consider the write-off of dormant receivables for more than 10 years in the books of accounts, in accordance with the procedures set forth by COA Circular No. 2016- 005; (iii) facilitate the liquidation of Advances for Payroll account and continue to trace the erroneous posting in the account and effect any adjustments, as necessary; (iv) recognize only the final and executory audit disallowances and charges in the account, Receivables – Disallowances/Charges; (v) update the records on the account Receivables – Disallowances/Charges and reconcile with the Audit Team’s records in order to make the necessary adjustment in the Agency’s books; (vi) continue identifying the reconciling items between the reciprocal accounts Due from/to Other Funds and effect the adjustments in the accounting records, as necessary; and (vii) institute a thorough monitoring of these reciprocal accounts by ensuring that entries made/posted from one account has a parallel recognition/posting to the other.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to enjoin the Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare and maintain SLs per creditor as necessary to support the balances presented in the GL and financial statements; and (ii) exhaust all remedies to gather the details and supporting documents needed for the identification of the amounts of dormant and long-outstanding payables to facilitate the reversion and/or write-off of such in accordance with the provisions of COA-DBM JC No. 1, s. 2021 and COA Circular No. 2016-005, and (iii) ensure that all financial transactions are accurately recorded and reviewed before posting to the General Ledger.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the a) Municipal Accountant to i) always secure a copy of MOA before recognizing equipment and/or facilities funded by inter-agency transfers; ii) make the necessary adjusting entry on the improper recognition of the two vehicles procured under KADIWA Financial grant Assistance Program; b) Municipal Treasurer to exert remedies as countermeasure to determine the source of fund deposited in the LGUs accounts in the absence of NADAI but not limited to making confirmations from Landbank; c) Officers and employees in-charge of the implementation of projects funded by other agencies to continue the timely implementation of projects based on the terms and conditions of the SAs to ensure the efficient delivery of public service to the intended beneficiaries; and d) Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to (i) maintain the good practice of prompt liquidation of fund transfers including the immediate refund of unutilized balance of the said funds; and (ii) update the records of all fund transfers from different agencies for proper monitoring and to establish the reliability of the year-end balance of the accounts.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the a)  BAC, together with the Technical Working Group (TWG), i) to take note/submit the lacking documents such as the BAC resolution declaring SCRB instead of LCRB, post-qualification evaluation reports, posting of Warranty Security, all under the provisions of R.A.9184 and its IRR ; ii) be apprised of the various intricacies of bidding and apply them accordingly to enhance the credibility of bidding process; and (iii) ensure that project contractors observe the proper size and details to be provided on project signboards including the update thereof to reflect the actual project status in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013; and (b) Accounting Office to check and ensure the completeness of documents before effecting payment.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the a) Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to (i) maintain/update the records of all fund transfers from different agencies for proper monitoring and to establish the reliability of the year-end balance of the accounts; and (ii) exhaust all remedies to gather the details and supporting documents needed for the identification of the amounts of dormant long-outstanding liabilities to facilitate the reversion and/or write -off.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (i) instruct the process owners of the Local Roads Asset Management System based on COA Circular 2015-008 to be mindful of their duties laid down in the said Circular; (ii) require the Supply/Property Officer to come up with the Local Road Inventory and Road Map and Local Road Network Property Card for all roads under the Agency’s jurisdiction; and (iii) require the Accounting Office to disclose information on Road Networks in the Notes to the FS in accordance with COA Circular 2015-008.

We recommended and the LCE agreed for the Municipal Accountant a) maintain the punctual submission of documents relating to CF; b) provide and clearly state in the DV the duration of implementation of the projects so that if the implementation of the project will extend to more than three months, additional amount may be granted only after liquidation of the previous cash advance; and c) employ a more efficient and timely release of cash advance for CF so as not to derail the prompt accomplishment and implementation of projects under the said fund.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (i) submit immediately the DVs with their supporting documents pertaining to the noted transactions; and (ii) strictly comply with the submission of DVs and supporting documents within the reglementary period as prescribed by COA Circular 2009-006.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) continue upholding its mandate in promoting local development by appropriating 20% of its annual NTA pursuant to the provisions of the LGC and DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 and JMC No. 01 dated November 04, 2020; and (b) Engineering Office and MPDO ensure timely delivery of the projects for CY 2023 and for every budget year, taking into account the legal rudiments of every project; (c) the Local Development Council (LDC), (i) continue to give precedence to projects focusing on inclusive growth and sustainable development of communities at the barangay level; and (ii) maintain the proactive involvement of the barangays thru the LDC in identifying socio-economic projects to be implemented out of the DF.

We recommended and the LCE agreed (a) require the BAC, together with the TWG, to prepare the BAC resolution declaring Single Calculated and Responsive Bidder (SCRB) if there is only one responsive bidder as provided by Section 36 of IRR of R.A. 9184; (b) ensure that all required postings in PhilGEPS as mentioned in the Revised IRR are complied within the period prescribed. Moreover, attach a printed copy of all postings to the related disbursement vouchers; (c) demand from the representative of the contractors a notarized formal document to establish that they are really an agent or an authorized representative of the winning bidder; (d) conduct the post-qualification exercise through significant verification based on the documents under examination so as to establish the integrity of documents and to detect misrepresentation; (c) require the Engineering Office to ensure that project contractors observe the proper size and details to be provided on project signboards including the update thereof to reflect the actual project status in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013; and (d) instruct the Accounting Office to check and ensure the completeness of documents and exert the proper diligence to discern that the documents submitted by the contractors are legitimate before effecting payment.

We reiterate our previous year’s recommendation and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) issue demand letters to all concerned AOs for them to liquidate their cash advances by submitting complete and valid settlement documents or refunding applicable portion of the cash advances, as necessary; and (ii) establish a thorough monitoring of cash advances granted to different Accountable Officers by making a schedule when these cash advances are due for liquidation. We also recommended the Accounting Office to reconcile regularly the GL and PRSCA and settle the discrepancy amounting to ₱892,217.68.

We recommended and the LCE agreed that the Management (a) direct the MSWMB to (i) to prioritize the review/update of the SWMP to ensure its sustainability, viability and effectiveness; (ii) prepare Long-term Action Plan and Annual Work Plan and Budget showing the SWM activities to be implemented and the financial counterpart for each SWM activity; and (iii) continue to improve the work of the monitoring and evaluation team and require them to prepare Progress Reports in line with the Annual Work Plan and the SWM Plan; and (b) consider the possibility of adding additional methods of processing waste material in the MRF to further reduce residual wastes.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the: a) Municipal Accountant to continue to (i) make necessary corrections/adjustments in affected inventory and expense accounts; (ii) observe the perpetual method in recognizing inventory and avoid recording outright expense when accounting for inventoriable goods and DRRM supplies; (iii) perform monthly reconciliation of records with Budget Office to reflect the accurate balances of obligations at a given period; (iv) utilize the LDRRMF only for projects that are directly related to disaster risk reduction and preparation/prevention in accordance with NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013; and(v) discuss and present the minimum required disclosure in the Notes to the FS as specified in COA Circular No. 2014-002; and b) Supply Officer, in coordination with concerned offices, to maintain Stock Cards and prepare and submit the SSMI and Inventory List of Items for DRRM to the Municipal Accountant for recording and adjusting related expense and inventory accounts.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) members of the BAC/TWG to (i) adhere to the provisions of the Revised IRR as to the completeness of documents in executing government contracts from requisition, bidding up to award; and (ii) submit the lacking documents presented in the table; (b) Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of documents before effecting payments; and (c) Property/Supply Officer to dispense with the redundant/unnecessary accounting forms and be guided with the Accounting Manual for LGAS.

We recommended and the LCE agreed that Management (a) continuously and constantly monitor, intensify and strategize collection efforts from taxpayers such as sending payment advisory or notice to taxpayers before the payment period or deadlines and the like; (b) instruct the Business Permit and Licensing Office (BPLO) and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to (i) remind the businesses/taxpayers on the submission of certified photocopies of their income tax returns (ITR) on or before April 30 of each year, as required by the Municipal Revenue Code and (ii) request from the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) the list of all BIR registered business establishments in Camiling, Tarlac with their declared gross sales before the start of the licensing period; (c) direct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to conduct the examination of books of accounts and pertinent records in accordance with Section 171 of the LGC, especially businesses/taxpayers who failed to submit their ITR; (d) instruct the Local Finance Committee to coordinate with the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) on the possible revision of the Municipal Revenue Code pursuant to Section 191 of the R.A. 7160; and (e) Municipal Accountant, to revisit the mapping of revenue accounts and record collected revenues to their proper revenue classification.

We recommended that the LCE to (i) continue to remit promptly and intact the loan amortization and mandatory contributions to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth within the prescribed period; (ii) exert extra effort in gathering pertinent documents and analyze the subject accounts to establish the accuracy of the balances in the inter-agency payables; and (iii) remit all the due premiums immediately, when evidence warrants.

We recommended and the LCE concurred that the Municipal Accountant and other concerned personnel enforce the immediate settlement of suspensions and disallowances, particularly those which have been rendered final and executory, in accordance with Sections 9.4 and 10.4, respectively, of the 2009 RRSA.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Treasurer to (i) furnish the updated RPTAR/ Taxpayer’s index card to Municipal Accountant at every beginning of the year for recording purposes; and (ii) to intensify and strategize tax collection efforts from taxpayers such as deployment of demand letters, posting of notice of delinquency in conspicuous places and tapping the assistance of the barangay officials for tax campaign to reach out to a larger area; and (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) establish RPT and SET receivables sourced from the list duly certified by the Treasury Office at the beginning of the year; (ii) make the necessary adjustments to account the proper treatment of advance tax collections and to correct the improper inclusions of penalties in the RPT/SET Receivable and Deferred Income accounts; (iii) exhaust all remedies to gather details for the adjustment of the unreconciled variances of RPT/SET receivables and deferred income accounts, after advance collection,  that were carried forward from prior years; and (iv) align the accounting procedures being adopted with the procedures provided by the MNGAS for LGUs with respect to recording of RPT transactions.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Inventory Committee, Accounting Office and the Municipal Treasurer/Supply Officer to (i) continue the conduct of annual physical count of PPE and submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year in accordance with Section 124 of MNGAS for LGUs; (ii) strongly consider availing the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts in accordance with COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and (iii) maintain updated Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, for all LGU properties and reconcile the noted discrepancies while taking into account the necessary adjustment in the books, as necessary.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare the corresponding adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the affected accounts; (ii) record transactions based on its nature/substance and according to the definition of accounts under the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs; and (b) MBO to further classify/break-down each Special Purpose Appropriations into different accounts based on the nature of the goods/services to be procured for each PPA.

We recommended and the LCE agreed, through the LDC, to (i) continue to prioritize projects focusing on inclusive growth and sustainable development of communities at the barangay level; and (ii) maintain the proactive involvement of the barangays in identifying socio-economic projects to be implemented out of the DF.

We recommended and the (i) LCE agreed to continue upholding its mandate in promoting local development by appropriating 20% of its annual IRA pursuant to the provisions of the LGC and DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017; and (ii) Engineering Office and MPDO assure the timely delivery of the projects for CY 2021 and for every budget year, taking into account the legal rudiments of every project.

We recommended and that the LCE agreed to require the (a) BAC and concerned officials in charge of procurement to continue to use the Checklist of Requirements and align its contents in accordance with the processes and documents established in R.A. No. 9184, infusing the observations noted by the Audit Team; (b) BAC, together with the TWG, to prepare the BAC resolution declaring LCRB only after post-qualification evaluation reports, require the submission of security bond by the LCB before contract execution, in compliance with R.A.No. 9184; (c) BAC and TWG to submit the lacking documents as enumerated in the discussion; (d) Engineering Office to (i) demand from the contractors Warranty Security before issuance of Certificate of Final Acceptance; and (ii) ensure that project contractors observe the proper size and details to be provided on project signboards including the update thereof to reflect the actual project status in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013; and (e) Accounting Office to check and ensure the completeness of documents before effecting payment.
The BAC commented that they will continuously improve in handling procurement processes with the help of the audit observations and recommendations by the Audit Team.
Furthermore, the BAC commented that the Warranty Security Bond will immediately be demanded from the contractors before issuance of Final and Acceptance for infrastructure projects.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) direct the MSWMB to (i) to review every two years or as the need arises, the SWMP for the purpose of ensuring its sustainability, viability and effectiveness; (ii) prepare an Annual Work Plan and Budget showing the SWM activities to be implemented and the financial counterpart for each SWM activity, taking into consideration the initiatives identified in the 10-year SWM Plan; and (iii) organize monitoring and evaluation team and require them to prepare Progress Reports in line with the Annual Work Plan and the SWM Plan; (b) consider reinforcing the MENRO to carve out more progress in the implementation of SWMP; and (c) require the cooperation of the Barangays in the implementation of Solid Waste Management particularly the establishment of functional MRF and campaign for proper waste segregation.

We recommended and the (a) LCE agreed to capacitate the GFPS and concerned officials with the mechanisms and processes in mainstreaming GAD  perspective in the LGU through trainings, conferences, symposia administered by the PCW, DILG or other NGAs directly involved in GAD concerns; (b) GFPS agreed to (i) update the GAD database by including sex-disaggregation and by obtaining other relevant GAD-related data from other sources; (ii) enhance the GPB and GAD AR by completely filling out the needed qualitative and quantitative aspects of the activities implemented including data on sex-disaggregation pursuant to MCW, as necessary; and (iii) equip themselves with the GeRL/HGDG tool and PIMME to determine how much of the Agency’s actual disbursements for implementing its major programs can be attributed to GAD.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) Accounting Office to (i) ensure that all disbursements are supported with complete documentation and that necessary data/information are provided before payment; (ii) submit the lacking documents; (iii) prepare the corresponding adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the affected accounts; (iv) record transactions based on its nature/substance and not just based on the account used for budget purposes; and (v) secure from the DepEd Accountant a certification confirming that all expenses chargeable against the SEF are net of the budgetary provision for the same or related item(s) in the DepEd budget; and (b) BAC to follow the prescribed necessary rules and regulations in the conduct of government procurement.; and (c) LSB to strive to fully utilize the SEF in accordance with the priority programs/projects to realize their foremost value for the benefit of stakeholders in the education sector.
The Municipal Accountant also replied that they will hold a discussion with the Budget Office pertaining to the appropriate account code to be used so as to harmonize the treatment on the LGU transactions in recording in its respective books/records. Moreover, erroneous entries as noted by the Audit Team in the amount of ₱64,690.25 were already adjusted as per JEV No. 221-2022-04-0007 dated April 29, 2022.
The Management also commented that while some projects/programs such as Educational Research and Sports Development were not included due to pandemic, they are still considering to allocate funds for these programs prospectively.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the concerned agency officials (i) allocate budget for insurance policies covering all insurable properties of the Municipality and to secure said policies from the GSIS so that the agency is protected and indemnified should calamities arise; and (ii) conduct a complete annual physical count of PPEs and Inventories and come up with the RPCPPE and RPCI and accordingly prepare PIF to be submitted to the Audit Team and GSIS to secure insurance.
The Management commented that they will prepare the PIF and outrightly comply with the insurance requirement upon completion of physical count of inventoriable items.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the a) Local Finance Committee (i) to design a systematic forecasting of revenue target by engaging a collaborated efforts from different Sections/Departments such as the BPLO and Assessor’s Office; and (ii) to identify additional sources of income and/or push for the passage of updated revenue code to boost local collection of taxes and fees; b) Municipal Accountant, aiding the Municipal Treasurer, to revisit the mapping of revenue accounts to align with the revenue categories per Annual Budget for more accurate comparison of the projected and actual income per sources as presented in the SCBAA; and c) Municipal Treasurer to constantly monitor, intensify and strategize collection efforts from taxpayers such as sending payment advisory or notice to taxpayers before the payment period or deadlines.
As to the mapping of revenue accounts, the Management commented that the MAO and the Treasury Office are working on a revised mapping of revenue accounts to facilitate an accurate variance analysis between actual and budgeted collections.

We recommended and the LCE agreed, in coordination with the SB, to (i) cause the formal constitution of clear guidelines on the provision of communication allowance by setting the parameters as to eligibility of those who may be qualified to the benefit and  the reasonable limit of the monthly entitlement, taking into consideration the value for money packages offered by telecommunication companies vis-à-vis the actual need to carry out official tasks; (ii) ensure the inclusion in the policy guidelines of a provision guaranteeing LGU’s ownership over the mobile phone units in the postpaid plans; and (iii) direct the concerned officials to submit the required certification of official calls and invoice/official receipts as part of the documents to support a claim for reimbursement/payment of communication expense.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (i) continue to remit promptly and intact the loan amortization and mandatory contributions to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth within the prescribed period; (ii) exert extra effort in gathering pertinent documents and analyze the subject accounts to establish the accuracy of the balances in the inter-agency payables; and (iii) remit all the due premiums immediately, when evidence warrants.

We recommended and the LCE concurred that the Municipal Accountant and other concerned personnel enforce the immediate settlement of suspensions and disallowances, particularly those which have been rendered final and executory, in accordance with Sections 9.4 and 10.4, respectively, of the 2009 RRSA.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct all concerned agency officials and personnel to submit immediately the lacking documents to the Audit Team and strictly observe the provisions of the previously mentioned regulations.

We recommended and (a) the head of the agency/office agreed to be mindful of the importance of issuing office order or authorization whenever employees are required to report for work on days when they are not supposed to, for the protection of the employees; (b) all concerned agency personnel agreed to immediately submit the duly approved DTRs; and (c) the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal HRMO, agreed to support the disbursement for payroll transactions with adequate documents for the efficient review and validation by the Audit Team and comply with the above provisions.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to i) send final demand letters to the concerned AOs for the liquidation of their accountabilities; and ii) require the concerned personnel to liquidate their cash advances as soon as the purpose thereof has been accomplished. We further recommended and the concerned personnel agreed to refund the per diems totaling ₱22,320.00 pertaining to the postponed trainings/seminars.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Treasury Office to update the RPTARs/ Taxpayers’ Index Card yearly, to include not only the land owner with updated payment but also those not paying their yearly land tax, and subsequently, prepare and furnish the Accounting Office the list required under Section 20, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs; and (b) Municipal Accountant to establish RPT and SET receivables sourced from the list duly certified by the Treasury Office at the beginning of the year. Further, we recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Assessor/ Project Manager to coordinate with the Provincial Project Manager for the conduct of training of municipal personnel concerned who are tasked to operate the PATAS so that a test run of the same be conducted immediately to evaluate the efficiency and effectiveness of the system.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct (a) the Inventory Committee to facilitate a one-time cleansing of PPE account balances through the implementation of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and (b) the Municipal Accountant i) to maintain an updated lapsing schedule showing correct computation of depreciation expense that will reconcile with the amounts recorded and presented in the FS; and ii) exert more effort to secure the necessary documents and reports so that adequate depreciation of all depreciable PPE items can be provided.

We recommended and the Budget Officer, Municipal Engineer, Municipal Planning Development Coordinator and other implementing offices agreed to strictly appropriate and utilize the 20% DF for only eligible or allowed development projects included in the list under DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1. Nevertheless, we commended the Management for efficiently implementing all the projects under its 20% DF for CY 2020.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) BAC together with the TWG to comply with the aforementioned rules and regulations of R.A. 9184; (b) Accounting Office to check and ensure the completeness of documents before effecting payment; and (c) concerned officials to submit the lacking documents as enumerated in the discussion.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) LDRRM Officer and concerned officials (i) to strictly conform with the provisions in preparing the necessary LDRRMFIP in prescribed format which includes the PPAs funded from LDRRMF current appropriation and from unexpended balance of the previous years transferred to the Special Trust Fund; and (ii) to update the LDRRMFIP for any changes related to COVID-19 PPAs and expenses; and (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare and submit the monthly report on sources and utilization of DRRMF covering all funds from local and different sources within the reglementary period; and (ii) discuss and present the minimum required disclosure in the Notes to the FS as specified in COA Circular No. 2014-002.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (i) BAC together with the TWG to comply with the stipulated requirements of R.A. 9184 and its Revised IRR in the procurement activities; (ii) Accounting Office to check and ensure the completeness and reliability of documents before effecting payment; and (iii) all concerned agency officials and personnel to submit immediately the lacking documents to the Audit Team and strictly observe the provisions of the abovementioned regulations.

We recommended and (a) the Management agreed to devise a mechanism on the utilization and disposition of funds obtained from GAA and donations for the achievement of the purpose to which the funds are intended; and (b) the concerned Municipal officials and employees agreed to prepare all the required reports in a timely manner and submit the same to the concerned oversight government agencies.

We recommended and the Management agreed to continue to (i) observe the process of incorporating at least 5% of its total Annual Budget for GAD PPAs; and (ii) perform the gender analysis of its GAD attributed PPAs through the administration of HGDG Test. Further, we reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and the LCE agreed to instruct the Agency GFPS personnel to (i) update its GAD Database to come up with more relevant information that will be useful in identifying gender issues and crafting GAD PPAs to effectively address said issues; and (ii) exert best effort to completely implement programmed GAD activities indicated in the GPB to achieve desired outcomes for the targeted beneficiaries.

We recommended and the Management agreed to (i) require the concerned personnel to submit regularly the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption; (ii) ensure that all disbursement vouchers for fuel consumption are supported with properly and completely accomplished DTTs in accordance with the prescribed format; and (iii) stop issuing fuel to privately owned vehicles and ensure that fuel is issued only to properly identified government vehicles and not to private vehicles.

We recommended and the Management agreed to (a) continue to remit promptly and intact the taxes due to BIR on or before 10th day of the following month; (b) design a more effective strategy that will result in the remittance of amount that is reconciled with actual taxes withheld; (c) adjust in the books the erroneous entry made by crediting back the Due to BIR account in the amount of ₱308,946.16; (d) exert extra effort in gathering pertinent documents and analyze the Due to BIR account to establish the accuracy of the balance; and (e) remit all the due taxes immediately, when evidence warrants.

We recommended and the Management agreed to (a) remit promptly and intact the loan amortization and mandatory contributions to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth within the prescribed period; (b) exert extra effort in gathering pertinent documents and analyze the subject accounts to establish the accuracy of the balances in the inter-agency payables; and (c) remit all the due premiums immediately, when evidence warrants. | Observation No. 1 AAR CY-2020

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive: (a) direct the Municipal Inventory Committee to i) conduct physical count of all PPE and label all properties/equipment showing complete description for easy identification and monitoring; ii) prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE); and iii) submit the same to COA; (b) require the Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer to maintain complete and updated ledger cards and property cards, respectively, and periodically conduct reconciliation thereon together with the RPCPPE; and (c) require the Municipal Accountant to i) prioritize the retrieval of records and supporting documents for the establishment of subsidiary ledgers for each class of PPE and for the preparation of the Registry of Public Infrastructures, so that general ledger balances of PPE including Public Infrastructure accounts are supported by independent and reliable records; ii) follow the guidelines for the depreciation of PPE; and iii) exert their best effort to secure the necessary documents and reports so that adequate depreciation can be provided.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a) send final demand letters to the concerned accountable officers for the liquidation of their accountabilities; b) stop the practice of granting new cash advances without the liquidation of previous ones; c) require the concerned personnel to liquidate their cash advances as soon as the purpose thereof has been accomplished; d) maintain individual Subsidiary Ledgers of all accountable officers for accurate monitoring of cash advances; and e) settle the difference of ₱73,005.40 between the records on cash advances and the GL account.
We recommended that management continue to comply with the provisions of loan agreement with the Land Bank of the Philippines.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: (a) the Market Supervisor to (i) execute immediately the Contracts of Lease with the lessees and strictly implement the terms and conditions stipulated therein; and (ii) enforce its rights such as issuance of closure order to stall owners with delinquent accounts and unrenewed business permits; and (b) the Municipal Treasurer to initiate productive measures and intensify collection of unpaid rentals including interests and surcharges.

We recommended that the: (a) Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee strictly adhere to the above-cited provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its RIRR in the procurement activities; (b) Municipal Accountant review thoroughly all claims presented for payment prior to approval to ensure that only properly approved and completely documented transactions/disbursements shall be paid; and (c) BAC Secretariat and other concerned agency officials and personnel submit immediately the lacking documents mentioned in the report to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the: (a) Management (i) maximize the utilization of the 20 percent development fund by fully implementing priority development projects identified pursuant to DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes; (ii) ensure that on-going projects are completed in 2020 as represented; and (iii) address the challenges encountered respecting the two projects that were not implemented during the year and exert best effort to implement the same if the need still persists; (b) Local Chief Executive (i) require all departments of the Municipality to prepare the annual PPMP for their procurement requirement; (ii) cause the BAC, the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Engineer and other officials concerned to submit the lacking documents mentioned in this report and remind the same to provide the Audit Team advanced copy of contracts/purchase orders as required in COA Circular No. 2009-01; (c) BAC, TWG and Secretariat strictly comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 9184 particularly concerning the timely approval of contract and issuance of Notice to Proceed to successful bidder to avoid delays in project execution, the posting of performance security by winning bidder with validity period clearly indicated therein, submission of BSD containing the required stipulations, passage of BAC Resolution supporting the establishment of TWG, and performance of authenticity check during post qualification evaluation;; and (d) Municipal Accountant check the completeness of supporting documents before effecting the payments of obligations in view of the contracts’ deliverables.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as chairman of the Municipal DRRM Council, cause the selection and accreditation of CSOs and private sector representative in accordance with the guidelines set forth in the aforementioned MC. We further recommended that he directs the (a) Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere to the above-cited provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its RIRR in the procurement activities; (b) Municipal Accountant to review thoroughly all claims presented for payment prior to approval to ensure that only properly approved and completely documented transactions/disbursements shall be paid; and (c) BAC Secretariat and other concerned agency officials and personnel submit immediately the lacking documents mentioned in the report to the Audit Team.

We recommended that Management instruct the Agency GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) personnel to (a) update the GAD Database by gathering data using the CBMS platform or other reliable data-gathering tools to come up with more relevant information that will be useful in identifying gender issues and crafting GAD PPAs to effectively address said issues; (b) attend trainings or workshops to obtain deeper understanding of HGDG test to better equip them in performing gender analysis, both at the planning and evaluation stages of GAD exercise; and (c) exert best effort to completely implement programmed GAD activities indicated in the GPB to achieve desired outcomes for the targeted beneficiaries.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to compel the MSWMB to (i) formulate a SWM Budget which will incorporate the program, plan and activities stated in the 10-Year SWMP; (ii) conduct a study regarding the commercial viability of the organic fertilizer produced by the composting facility to attain a reduced volume of residual wastes generated in the municipality; (iii) strictly implement the “no segregation, no collection” policy of the Municipality; and (iv) ensure the speedy implementation of Ordinance No. 13 S. 2006 through the creation of SWM Task Force consisting of PNP personnel and barangay officials as enforcers of the local law so that cases of open burning of solid wastes and littering are restrained, immediately reported and acted upon.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to a) strictly adhere with the provisions of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009 and P.D. No. 1445; and b) diligently monitor the renewal of fidelity bonds of the agency’s accountable officers yearly before the expiration of the present bond.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Treasury and Accounting Offices to submit, within the timeframe prescribed in the above COA Circular and Presidential Decree, disbursement vouchers with complete supporting documents.  Likewise, we reiterated our request for the submission of the disbursement vouchers with supporting documents corresponding to the above stated issued checks to prevent audit suspension of transactions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Human Resource and Management Officer to (a) gather all pertinent documents and analyze the subject accounts to establish the accuracy of the balances in the Due to GSIS, Due to PhilHealth and Due to Pag-IBIG accounts; and (b) remit all the due premiums immediately, when evidence warrants. | AAR 2019

No. 1445; and ii) diligently monitor the renewal of fidelity bonds of the agency’s accountable officers yearly before the expiration of the present bond.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Treasury and Accounting Offices to submit, within the timeframe prescribed in the above COA Circular and Presidential Decree, disbursement vouchers with complete supporting documents.  Likewise, we reiterated our request for the submission of the disbursement vouchers with supporting documents corresponding to the stated issued checks to prevent audit suspension of transactions.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the a) Accounting and Treasury Offices to i) continue to withhold and remit taxes to the BIR in compliance with existing tax rules and regulations and ii) design effective strategy that will result in the remittance of amount that is reconciled with actual taxes withheld; and b) the Accounting Office to i) gather all pertinent documents and analyze the Due to BIR account to establish the accuracy of the balance; and ii) remit all the due taxes immediately, when evidence warrants.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Human Resource and Management Officer to (i) gather all pertinent documents and analyze the subject accounts to establish the accuracy of the balances in the Due to GSIS, Due to PhilHealth and Due to Pag-IBIG accounts; and (ii) remit all the due premiums immediately, when evidence warrants.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Treasurer, take the necessary steps/actions to immediately comply with the requirements on maintaining bank accounts with private bank, otherwise cause the transfer of the deposited funds to any of the authorized depository banks.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct.

a.	the Municipal Treasurer to: (i) initiate productive measures and intensify collection of delinquent taxes and unpaid rentals in order to meet the target revenue collection, and (ii) coordinate with the Market Administrator in establishing a simple electronic database system which will monitor the payment of stall rentals, including goodwill/occupancy fees and promptly detect delinquencies; and

b.	the Market Supervisor to execute immediately the Contracts of Lease with the lessees and strictly enforce the terms and conditions to generate additional income for the Municipality and validly enforce its rights in case of stall owner’s default.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee to: (a) consider the timelines set for the project and improve its monitoring functions or supervision for projects being implemented to avoid incurrence of delays; (b) henceforth, ensure that similar projects are included in the Annual Procurement Plan of the Municipality as required by DILG Memo Circular 2018-01; and (c) submit to the Auditor the copy of contracts within five days from perfection thereof.

We recommended that:

a.	The Municipal Accountant check the completeness of supporting documents before effecting the payments of obligations in view of the contracts’ deliverables; and

b.	the Local Chief Executive direct (i) the Bids and Awards Committee and its Secretariat Office and the Municipal Engineer to submit the lacking documents, and to observe the instructions on contract submission prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-01, (ii) BAC to follow the provision on observers’ invitation as provided in RA No. 9184 and to ensure the performance of the post-qualification process.

c.	The Municipal Engineer adopt strict measures in the supervision during project implementation and inspection of completed projects to minimize, if not avoid the occurrence of defects and to ensure acceptable quality output or deliverables delivered or transferred by the contractors.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:

a.	MDRRM Officer to ensure the proper presentation with corresponding allocation of fund for each of the activities in the LDRRMF Investment Plan, including those funded from unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years and to utilize the LDRRMF only for purposes for which the fund was appropriated;

b.	Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere with the guidelines and procedures provided by RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR in the conduct of procurement; and 

c.	BAC, its Secretariat and the Accounting Office to submit the lacking documents and disbursement vouchers stated above.

We recommended that the LCE instruct:

b.	the MENRO officer and support offices to strictly implement the provisions of Municipal Ordinance No. 13-2006, specifically in the proper segregation of wastes in order to instill discipline among constituents and attain the objectives RA No. 9003.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive instruct the:

a.	GAD Focal Point System to lead, monitor and fast track the implementation of GAD projects, programs and activities to ensure that GAD Budget is fully utilized to achieve its objectives and administer HGDG test for attributed GAD PPAs;

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) instruct the Municipal Accountant to support the disbursements for payroll with adequate documents; and (b) in coordination with the Municipal HRMO and Sangguniang Bayan adopt and implement clear policies and guidelines in hiring and proper monitoring of Contracts of Services/Job Orders, to conform with the existing regulations.

We recommended that: a) the Local Chief Executive refrain from granting cash advances for expenses which could be paid thru check directly to the supplier; b) the BAC adhere with the guidelines for alternative mode of procurement provided in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; c) the Municipal Accountant check the completeness of supporting documents before recording the liquidation of cash advances; and d) the concerned personnel to: i) submit the lacking documents and ii) refund the excessive and unnecessary expenses in the amount of P53,410.00 as stated above.
